text: The video shows an area in the descending colon with evidence of a previous perforation, characterized by visible scarring or tissue changes.

The surgeon clears the mesentery up to the margin, ensuring no false ICG signals by cauterizing upon observing the green fluorescence. The proximal margin is then transected.

The surgeon clears the mesentery up to the proximal margin, ensuring no false ICG signals indicate good blood supply where there is none. The mesentery is cauterized upon observing the green ICG signal to mark the margins, followed by transecting the proximal margin.
The surgeon prepares to transect the rectum, indicating a preference for manual techniques over stapling for transrectal extraction in benign disease cases.
The surgeon proceeds to transect the rectum, following the lateral mobilization and posterior dissection to enhance mobility for transrectal extraction.

The surgeon cleans the surgical field and prepares for a single-layer sewn anastomosis. A silk suture is used to stabilize the bowel, visible posteriorly, preventing movement during the procedure. The surgeon begins sewing from the posterior side, moving around the far corner, and then towards the near side, ensuring good tissue perfusion and secure suture bites.

The surgeon cleans the surgical field and prepares for a single-layer sewn anastomosis. A silk suture is used to align the proximal and distal bowel, visible posteriorly, to prevent movement during the procedure. The anastomosis begins with sewing from the posterior side, extending around the far corner.
The surgeon begins sewing the anastomosis on the near side, carefully checking perfusion to ensure good blood supply and taking precise bites with the suture.
The surgeon meticulously imbricates the anterior row of sutures, ensuring proper alignment by alternating between two sutures to secure the tissue.

The surgeon meticulously imbricates the anterior row of the anastomosis, ensuring precise alignment of the tissue layers. Two sutures are used to secure the anterior aspect, with careful back-and-forth stitching visible.
